title: Markhamia hildebrandtii

intro: Markhamia hildebrandtii, also known as the African bloodflower, is a deciduous tree native to Madagascar. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Markhamia hildebrandtii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Markhamia hildebrandtii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues in traditional medicine.
